name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer and financial services network with 88+ locations across Miami, offering cash transfers, bill payments, and money orders through partner retailers.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer service that operates through a network of retail partner locations rather than standalone branches. The company functions as a financial services platform, partnering with existing retailers like CVS pharmacies, supermarkets, and independent check-cashing businesses to provide accessible financial services across Miami and beyond.

MoneyGram's core offerings include domestic and international money transfers, allowing customers to send and receive money both online and offline. Beyond transfers, they provide check-cashing services, money orders, and bill payment options. The service is designed for convenience, with customers able to access locations during extended retail hours—many CVS locations stay open until 11 PM or later, while independent partners have varying hours. The company emphasizes ease of use and multiple access channels.

What distinguishes MoneyGram is its massive retail distribution network. Rather than maintaining their own locations, they leverage partnerships with major retailers (CVS, supermarkets, multiservice centers) to achieve high accessibility. This model keeps overhead low and makes services available in diverse neighborhoods. The 88 Miami locations represent significant market penetration, with options ranging from busy commercial corridors to residential areas. Their dual online and in-person service model provides flexibility.

However, MoneyGram's primary focus remains serving unbanked and underbanked populations through basic financial transactions rather than credit-building or wealth-management solutions. While convenient, the network model means service quality and hours depend entirely on partner retailers. International transfer fees, exchange rates, and other cost structures are not disclosed on the location finder page, making it impossible to assess competitiveness. This is a transaction-focused service for customers needing immediate cash movement or money orders, not a comprehensive financial solution.
pros: 88+ locations across Miami provide extensive geographic coverage and accessibility
Extended hours through CVS partners (many open until 11 PM or 11:59 PM) enable after-hours access
Dual online and offline service options allow flexibility in how customers conduct transfers
Partnership model with supermarkets and multiservice centers serves diverse neighborhoods including lower-income areas
No requirement to maintain separate account—pay-as-you-go transaction model
Established brand with global money transfer network for international remittances
Multiple location types (pharmacies, supermarkets, independent agencies) reduce travel time for most customers
cons: Service quality and hours entirely dependent on partner retailers, with no guarantee of consistency
Website provides no pricing information, fees, or exchange rates—complete opacity on costs
Physical transfer still requires visiting a location; online option details not explained on location page
No credit-building benefits—purely transactional with no account history reporting
Limited to money movement and basic services; cannot address underlying financial challenges
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing to send/receive money without a bank account
International immigrants or travelers sending remittances home
Customers needing immediate check cashing or money orders without bank delays
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers in Miami who need quick money transfers, check cashing, or money orders without maintaining a bank account. The primary caveat is complete pricing opacity—fees, exchange rates, and transfer costs are not disclosed on their website, making it impossible to compare value before transacting.
services: Retail partner network access (CVS, supermarkets, independent agencies)
